title: Vanderbilt's Half-Court Heave Falls Short, Sending Nebraska to NCAA Sweet 16 - ESPN Recap
body:
In a nail-biting finish to the second round of the NCAA tournament, the Nebraska Cornhuskers edged out Vanderbilt in a heart-stopping 74-72 victory. The game's closing seconds saw Nebraska forward Braden Frager seemingly seal the win with a clutch play, only for Tyler Tanner's half-court heave to rim out at the buzzer, sending the Cornhuskers to the Sweet 16.

The tension in the Oklahoma City arena was palpable as the clock ticked down, with both teams trading baskets and neither able to gain a clear advantage. It was during this intense stretch that Frager made a move that would have been etched in Cornhusker lore forever, had it not been for Tanner's desperate attempt to salvage the game. Frager stole the inbounds pass with 9 seconds remaining, and in a display of quick thinking and athleticism, he launched a deep pass to his teammate. The resulting layup attempt was good, putting Nebraska up by 2 points with just a few seconds left on the clock.

However, Vanderbilt was not about to go down without a fight. The Commodores drove the length of the court and forced Nebraska into a frantic scramble to get back on defense. As time expired, Tanner was left with only one option – a half-court shot that would either be the game-winner or the buzzer-beater that would send his team packing. The Cornhusker faithful held their collective breath as Tanner launched the shot, only to watch in horror as it rimmed out, securing the win for Nebraska.

The Cornhuskers' victory was a testament to their resilience and determination, as they overcame a series of early setbacks to come out on top. Nebraska had struggled with consistency throughout the game, but their ability to adapt and make crucial plays when it mattered most proved to be the difference-maker. For Vanderbilt, the loss marked a disappointing end to a promising tournament run, one that had seen the Commodores overcome significant adversity to reach the second round.

As Nebraska prepares to face their next opponent in the Sweet 16, they will be looking to build on the momentum generated by this thrilling win. The Cornhuskers have shown they have the ability to compete with the best teams in the country, and now they will look to prove it against an even tougher opponent. For Vanderbilt, the focus will shift to regrouping and reassessing their approach, as they look to bounce back from this tough loss.

The Cornhuskers' Sweet 16 appearance marks a significant milestone for the program, and it will be interesting to see how they respond to the increased pressure and scrutiny that comes with advancing to the tournament's later stages. As they look ahead to their next challenge, Nebraska will be buoyed by the knowledge that they have the skills and the determination to compete with the best teams in the country. With their confidence boosted by this thrilling win, the Cornhuskers will be looking to make a deep run in the tournament and bring a championship to Lincoln.
